pattern.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (GS Paper I + CSAT Paper II)
pattern.stage_2: Mains – Written (9 papers: Essay, GS I–IV, Optional I–II, Language papers)

remarks: 2025 papers pending official upload; CSAT is qualifying

pattern.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (shared with CSE Prelims)
pattern.stage_2: Mains – Written (6 papers: General English, General Knowledge, 2 Optional subjects × 2 papers each)

remarks: Prelims is common with CSE; separate Mains for IFoS

pattern.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (Paper I: GS & Engg. Aptitude; Paper II: Engineering discipline)
pattern.stage_2: Mains – Written (Paper I & Paper II: Engineering discipline, conventional/descriptive)

remarks: Pattern revised from 2017 onwards (Prelims added); 2015–2016 had only Written + Interview

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (English, GK, Elementary Mathematics; OTA: English + GK only)
pattern.stage_2: SSB Interview (conducted by MoD, not UPSC)

remarks: Two cycles per year; Mathematics paper not required for OTA entry

exam_name: National Defence Academy & Naval Academy (NDA & NA)

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (Mathematics + General Ability Test)
pattern.stage_2: SSB Interview (conducted by MoD, not UPSC)

remarks: Two cycles per year; females eligible from 2022 onwards (SC order)

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (Paper I & Paper II: clinical medicine, surgery, gynaecology, etc.)
pattern.stage_2: Personality Test / Interview

remarks: Single-day written exam (both papers); no Mains written stage

exam_name: Indian Economic Service / Indian Statistical Service (IES/ISS)

pattern.stage_1: Written – Both (General English & General Studies: MCQ; Economics/Statistics papers: Written/Descriptive)
pattern.stage_2: Personality Test / Interview

remarks: IES: 6 papers (Gen. English, Gen. Studies, Economics I–IV); ISS: 6 papers (Gen. English, Gen. Studies, Statistics I–IV)

not_conducted: None (conducted every year; exam renamed/restructured in 2020 from Geologist Exam)

pattern.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (Paper I: General Studies; Paper II: discipline-specific)
pattern.stage_2: Mains – Written (3 discipline-specific descriptive papers)

remarks: Renamed from 'Geologist Examination' to 'Combined Geo-Scientist Examination' from 2020; papers for 2015–2019 are under old name

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (General English, Indian Freedom Struggle, Current Events, Indian Polity, General Accounting Principles, Industrial Relations & Labour Laws, etc.)
pattern.stage_2: Interview / Skill Test

remarks: Not conducted every year; recruitment driven by vacancy notifications; 2020 exam held in 2021

exam_name: Central Armed Police Forces – Assistant Commandant (CAPF AC)

pattern.stage_1: Written – Both (Paper I: MCQ – General Ability & Intelligence; Paper II: Descriptive – Essay, Précis, Comprehension)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Standards / Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

remarks: Paper I is MCQ; Paper II is descriptive/written — both on same day

pattern.stage_1: Tier I – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude, English Comprehension)
pattern.stage_2: Tier II – MCQ (Paper I: Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning; Paper II: English Language & Comprehension; Paper III: Statistics / General Studies – Finance & Economics as applicable)
pattern.stage_3: Tier III – Written/Descriptive (Essay, Letter, Precis in English/Hindi) + Skill Test / Document Verification

remarks: Pattern revised in 2022: Tier III is now descriptive pen-and-paper; Tier IV (Skill Test/CPT) is qualifying. Prior to 2022, 4 Tiers existed. 2020 exam delayed to 2021 due to COVID-19.

pattern.stage_1: Tier I – MCQ (General Intelligence, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, General Awareness)
pattern.stage_2: Tier II – Both: Section I MCQ (Math + Reasoning + English + GK) + Section II Skill Test / Typing Test (qualifying)

remarks: Pattern revised in 2023: Tier II now includes MCQ sections plus Skill/Typing test in same sitting. Previously Tier II was descriptive. 2020 exam delayed to 2021.

not_conducted: 2016 – Not conducted separately (merged cycle); 2020 – Deferred to 2021 (COVID-19)

pattern.stage_1: Paper I – MCQ (Numerical & Mathematical Ability, Reasoning & Problem Solving, General Awareness, English Language & Comprehension)
pattern.stage_2: Paper II – Written/Descriptive (Short Essay / Letter in English or any language in 8th Schedule) — Qualifying only

remarks: Paper II is qualifying (marks not counted in merit). Pattern revised in 2021: Paper I is now fully MCQ-based computer exam. Havaldar posts included from 2022.

not_conducted: 2016 – Not conducted; 2017 – Not conducted; 2022 – Not conducted (merged with 2021 cycle)

pattern.stage_1: CBT (Computer Based Test) – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Standard Test (PST) – Qualifying

remarks: Recruitment is need/vacancy based; not held every year. 2018-19 recruitment was a mega cycle. PET/PST conducted by CAPFs/SSB, not SSC.

pattern.stage_1: Paper I – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, Part A: General Engineering – Civil/Electrical/Mechanical as per post)
pattern.stage_2: Paper II – Written/Descriptive (Conventional: Civil & Structural / Electrical / Mechanical Engineering as per post)

remarks: Streams: Civil & Structural, Electrical, Mechanical. Paper II is conventional/descriptive. 2020 delayed to 2021. Both papers on same day in recent years.

exam_name: CPO – Sub-Inspector (SI) in Delhi Police / CAPFs

pattern.stage_1: Paper I – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude, English Comprehension)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Endurance Test (PET) / Medical Exam – Qualifying
pattern.stage_3: Paper II – MCQ (English Language & Comprehension) + Final Medical

remarks: Paper I and Paper II both MCQ (CBT). PET between Paper I and II is qualifying. Final merit based on Paper I + Paper II combined.

pattern.stage_1: Written Exam – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, English Language & Comprehension)
pattern.stage_2: Skill Test – Stenography Speed Test (80 wpm for Grade D; 100 wpm for Grade C) – Qualifying

remarks: Skill test is qualifying; final merit on written exam marks only. Grade C: Group 'B' Non-Gazetted; Grade D: Group 'C'. 2020 delayed to 2021.

not_conducted: Phase I–III data before 2016 not in scope; no missing phases from Phase IV onwards
paper_availability: Phase IV (2016), Phase V (2017), Phase VI (2018), Phase VII (2019), Phase VIII (2020), Phase IX (2021), Phase X (2022), Phase XI (2023), Phase XII (2024–25)

pattern.stage_1: CBT – MCQ (General Intelligence, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, General Awareness; difficulty varies by post – Matriculation / Higher Secondary / Graduation level)
pattern.stage_2: Document Verification / Skill Test (post-specific, qualifying)

remarks: One phase per year. No written/descriptive stage; purely CBT MCQ. Post categories vary: 10th pass, 12th pass, or Graduate level posts notified together.

not_conducted: None (DEO is a post under CHSL; conducted every year with CHSL)

pattern.stage_1: Tier I – MCQ (same as CHSL: General Intelligence, English, Quantitative Aptitude, General Awareness)
pattern.stage_2: Tier II – MCQ + DEO Skill Test (Data Entry Speed Test: 8,000 key depressions/hour – Qualifying)

remarks: DEO is not a separate exam — it is a post category within CHSL. Skill test speed requirement: 8,000 key depressions/hour for DEO; 15,000 for DEO Grade A (CAG). Conducted alongside LDC/JSA posts.

not_conducted: 2016, 2017, 2018 – Not conducted (2015 cycle results pending); 2022 – Not conducted (2021 cycle ongoing); 2024 – Not conducted (2024 notification issued, exam pending in 2025)
paper_availability: 2015–16 cycle, 2019–20 cycle (CBT 1 & 2 papers available), 2021–22 cycle (CBT 1 & 2 papers available)

pattern.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness)
pattern.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness – higher difficulty; post-specific)
pattern.stage_3: Skill Test / Typing Test / CBAT (post-specific, qualifying) + Document Verification

remarks: Mega recruitment cycles: 2015–16 (35,000+ posts), 2019–21 (35,208 posts), 2024–25 (11,558 posts notified). Not an annual exam — cycle-based. Graduate & Undergraduate post categories notified together. 2022 protest-related re-exam controversy.

not_conducted: 2015, 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, 2023 – Not conducted (gap between 2018-19 cycle and 2024 cycle)

pattern.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, General Science)
pattern.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ (Physics & Chemistry, Basics of Computers & Applications, Basics of Environment & Pollution Control, Technical Abilities – discipline specific)

remarks: Disciplines: Civil, Electrical, Mechanical, Electronics & Communication, IT, Depot Material Superintendent, Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant, etc. CBT 2 is discipline-specific. Recruitment is vacancy-driven, not annual.

not_conducted: 2015, 2016, 2017 – Not conducted (2018 mega cycle covered backlog); 2020, 2021 – Not conducted (2018 cycle results still processing); 2023 – Not conducted; 2025 – Notification issued, exam pending
paper_availability: 2018–19 cycle (CBT papers available), 2022 cycle (CBT papers available)

pattern.stage_1: CBT – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Science, General Awareness & Current Affairs)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) – Qualifying (running, weight lifting tasks gender/category specific)

remarks: Mega cycle 2018–19: 1,03,769 posts — largest single RRB recruitment. 2022 cycle: 1,03,769 posts again. No written descriptive stage. PET is qualifying only. Minimum 10th pass eligibility.

not_conducted: 2015, 2016, 2017 – Not conducted (2018 mega cycle); 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023 – Not conducted (2018 cycle results & 2024 notification gap)
paper_availability: 2018–19 cycle (CBT 1 & 2 Part A & B papers available), 2024 cycle (ongoing/pending)

pattern.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Science, General Awareness & Current Affairs)
pattern.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ: Part A (General topics) + Part B (Trade/Discipline specific — qualifying)
pattern.stage_3: CBAT (Computer Based Aptitude Test, qualifying) + Document Verification + Medical

remarks: ALP and Technician notified together in same cycle. CBT 2 Part B (trade syllabus) is qualifying — marks not counted in merit. CBAT required only for ALP post. ITI/Diploma required for eligibility.

not_conducted: 2015, 2016, 2017 – Not conducted (2018 mega cycle); 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023 – Not conducted (gap years)

pattern.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Science, General Awareness & Current Affairs)
pattern.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ (General topics + Trade/Discipline specific) — for Grade 1 only; Grade 3 shortlisted from CBT 1 only

remarks: Technician Grade 1 requires CBT 2 (discipline-specific); Grade 3 does not. Notified alongside ALP in same recruitment cycle. ITI qualification required.

not_conducted: 2015, 2016, 2017 – Not conducted (2018 mega cycle); 2020, 2021, 2022 – Not conducted (2018 cycle + gap); 2025 – Notification issued, exam pending

pattern.stage_1: CBT – MCQ (General Awareness, Arithmetic, General Intelligence & Reasoning)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Measurement Test (PMT) – Qualifying

remarks: Conducted by Railway Recruitment Boards under Railway Protection Force. Mega cycle 2018: 9,739 posts. 2024 cycle: 4,660 posts. PET includes 1600m/800m run, long jump, high jump (gender specific).

not_conducted: 2015, 2016, 2017 – Not conducted (2018 mega cycle); 2020, 2021, 2022 – Not conducted (gap years); 2025 – Notification issued, exam pending

pattern.stage_1: CBT – MCQ (General Awareness, Arithmetic, General Intelligence & Reasoning)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Measurement Test (PMT) – Qualifying

remarks: Graduate-level eligibility (unlike Constable which is 10th pass). Same CBT pattern as RPF Constable but separate merit list. 2018 cycle: 1,120 posts. 2024 cycle: 452 posts. Notified alongside RPF Constable.

pattern.stage_1: AFCAT Written – MCQ (General Awareness, Verbal Ability in English, Numerical Ability, Reasoning & Military Aptitude) + EKT – MCQ for Technical branch (Engineering Knowledge Test: Mechanical / Electronics & Communication / Electrical & Instrumentation)
pattern.stage_2: AFSB Interview (Air Force Selection Board: Stage 1 – OIR & PPDT; Stage 2 – Psychological tests, GTO, Interview)

remarks: Two cycles per year (Feb & Aug approximately). EKT only for Technical branch candidates. Flying branch candidates do CPSS (Computerised Pilot Selection System) at AFSB. Commissioned officer entry. NDA/CDSE entries are separate from AFCAT.

exam_name: Indian Navy SSR (Senior Secondary Recruit – Sailor)
not_conducted: 2020-Aug – Cancelled (COVID-19); 2023 – Recruitment moved to Agniveer SSR scheme only; SSR as a standalone entry discontinued after 2022
paper_availability: 2015 to 2022 (Feb & Aug cycles; papers available online)

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (English, Science, Mathematics, General Knowledge)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Fitness Test (PFT) – Qualifying

remarks: SSR entry discontinued as a separate scheme after Agnipath rollout (2022). From 2022–23 onwards, replaced by Agniveer SSR. 10+2 PCM eligibility. No interview stage. Selection purely on written merit + PFT.

not_conducted: 2020-Aug – Cancelled (COVID-19); 2023 – Recruitment moved to Agniveer MR/SSR scheme; AA as standalone entry discontinued after 2022
paper_availability: 2015 to 2022 (Feb & Aug cycles; papers available online)

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (English, Science, Mathematics, General Knowledge)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Fitness Test (PFT) – Qualifying

remarks: AA entry discontinued as a separate scheme after Agnipath rollout (2022). From 2022–23 onwards, integrated into Agniveer schemes. 10+2 PCM eligibility. Higher technical aptitude requirement than SSR. No interview stage.

paper_availability: 2022 (Army CEE, Navy, AF papers available), 2023 (papers available), 2024 (papers available)

pattern.stage_1: Army: CEE – MCQ (General Knowledge, General Science, Maths, Reasoning – trade/category specific) Navy Agniveer SSR/MR: MCQ (Science, Maths, English, GK) AF Agniveer: MCQ (Reasoning & General Awareness + Phase 2 trade test)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Fitness Test (PFT) / Physical Measurement – Qualifying (all three services)

remarks: Agnipath Scheme launched June 2022 replacing regular soldier/sailor/airman recruitment. 4-year service tenure; 25% retained permanently. Army has category-wise CEE (Sol GD, Sol Tech, Sol Clerk, Sol NA, etc.). Navy: Agniveer SSR & MR. AF: Agniveer Vayu. Separate notifications per service. Scheme under political review as of 2024–25.

not_conducted: 2020 – Deferred to 2021 (COVID-19); some years only 1 cycle instead of 2

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (Paper I: Reasoning & Elementary Mathematics; Paper II: General Knowledge & English)
pattern.stage_2: SSB Interview (Services Selection Board: Stage 1 – OIR & PPDT; Stage 2 – Psych, GTO, Interview)

remarks: Graduate-level entry for civilians already employed. Candidates must be employed / self-employed. Not a full-time military service — part-time commissioned officer role. Exam conducted by HQ Integrated Defence Staff. Two papers, both MCQ.

paper_availability: 2015–16 to 2024 (batch-wise papers available; some shifts memory-based)

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (English, Science, Maths, Reasoning, GK / Current Affairs — Navik GD & DB; additional technical for Yantrik)
pattern.stage_2: Physical Fitness Test (PFT) + Physical Measurement Test – Qualifying

remarks: Three posts: Navik General Duty (10+2 PCM), Navik Domestic Branch (10th pass), Yantrik (Diploma in Mech/Electrical/Electronics Engg). Yantrik written includes technical subjects. Recruited under Indian Coast Guard, Ministry of Defence. Two cycles/year approximately.

not_conducted: 2020 – Deferred to 2021 (COVID-19); 2022 – Limited vacancies, delayed notification

pattern.stage_1: Written – MCQ (General English, General Intelligence, General Science, Nursing & related subjects)
pattern.stage_2: Interview / Psychological Assessment (Army HQ)

remarks: B.Sc. Nursing eligibility required. Commissioned as Lieutenant in Army Medical Corps. Conducted by Army HQ / DGAFMS. Female candidates only. Approx 200–400 vacancies per year. Bond period applies after selection.

pattern.stage_1: Prelims MCQ (QA, Res, Eng)
pattern.stage_2: Mains MCQ + Descriptive

pattern.stage_1: Prelims MCQ
pattern.stage_2: Mains MCQ

pattern.stage_1: Prelims MCQ
pattern.stage_2: Mains MCQ + Descriptive

pattern.stage_1: Prelims MCQ
pattern.stage_2: Mains MCQ

not_conducted: 2020 (COVID – held in 2021) No other major gaps

pattern.stage_1: Phase II: MCQ (Mains – Obj.)
pattern.stage_2: — (No Stage 3; merit-based)

not_conducted: 2020 (COVID – held in 2021) No other major gaps

pattern.stage_1: — (No Stage 2)
pattern.stage_2: — (No Stage 3)

exam_name: IBPS RRB Officer Scale-III (Variant 5 – Senior Manager)
not_conducted: 2020 (COVID – held in 2021) No other major gaps

pattern.stage_1: — (No Stage 2)
pattern.stage_2: — (No Stage 3)

exam_name: Multiple Choice / Objective type – computer-based or OMR
Conventional written answers, essay, or descriptive papers (pen & paper)
Combination of MCQ (Part A) + Descriptive (Part B) in same paper
Most exams deferred / cancelled in 2020; cycles resumed in 2021
